audit_items.proof_trace: `ber_sockbuf_alloc()` at line 78 can return NULL on heap allocation failure (confirmed in sockbuf.c:62), but `sb` is passed directly to `ber_sockbuf_add_io()` at line 80 with no null guard; `ber_sockbuf_add_io()` begins with `assert(sb != NULL)` (sockbuf.c:180), which aborts the process on NULL. Sibling program `etest.c` checks `sb == NULL` after the same call pattern, confirming the guard was intended but omitted in `dtest.c`.
audit_items.attack_scenario: On a memory-constrained host where `ber_sockbuf_alloc()` returns NULL, invoking `dtest` (e.g., piping attacker-supplied BER data to stdin) triggers the assertion abort in `ber_sockbuf_add_io`, crashing the process before any data is parsed.
